title: Bateson Learning Levels PASS: strict invariant filtration on S_9 with witnesses at every tier
_fixture_purpose: Certifies Bateson's Learning Levels (0/I/II/III) as a strict invariant filtration on QA state spaces. Four invariants (orbit, family, modulus, ambient category) define five operator classes (L_0, L_1, L_2a, L_2b, L_3). Tiered Reachability Theorem exhaustively verified on S_9: 81+1712+3456+1312 = 6561 pairs. Only 26% Level-I reachable — quantitative double bind.
source_attribution: Gregory Bateson, 'Steps to an Ecology of Mind' (1972), logical types hierarchy from Russell & Whitehead 'Principia Mathematica'. Requisite variety: W. Ross Ashby, 'An Introduction to Cybernetics' (1956). QA formalization: Will Dale + Claude, 2026-04-04. Full theoretical sketch: docs/theory/QA_BATESON_LEARNING_LEVELS_SKETCH.md. Exhaustive verification: tools/verify_bateson_double_bind.py.

invariant_filtration.description: Orbit-preserving operators: phi(s) in Orbit(s) for all s in S_m. Includes all powers of T and intra-orbit automorphisms. The Level-I reachable set R_1(s_0) equals Orbit(s_0).
Orbit-changing, family-preserving operators: sends some orbit to a distinct orbit while preserving cosmos/satellite/singularity family classification.
Family-changing, modulus-preserving operators: crosses cosmos/satellite/singularity boundaries while staying in S_m.
Modulus-changing operators: maps S_m -> S_n with m != n. Includes reductions (mod 9 -> mod 3) and extensions (mod 9 -> mod 24 via CRT).

tiered_reachability_theorem.statement: For s_0, s_* in S_m, the minimum tier tau(s_0, s_*) required to reach s_* from s_0 via operators of class L_0 union ... union L_k is determined by which invariant separates source from target: tau=0 iff s_0=s_*; tau=1 iff s_* in Orbit(s_0); tau=2a iff different orbit, same family; tau=2b iff different family, same modulus; tau=3 iff different modulus.
tiered_reachability_theorem.key_lemma: R_1(s_0) = Orbit(s_0). Proof: T in L_1 generates the orbit; every L_1 operator preserves orbit membership by definition; hence any composition of L_1 operators applied to s_0 remains in Orbit(s_0).
tiered_reachability_theorem.proof_sketch: Lower bound: each tier is characterized by preserving a specific invariant (orbit, family, modulus). Operators below that tier preserve the invariant; targets breaking the invariant are unreachable. Upper bound: piecewise construction phi(s_0)=s_*, phi|_{S_m\{s_0}}=id achieves the minimum tier since the only family/orbit change is at s_0 itself.

quantitative_double_bind.interpretation: Under the default QA dynamic T alone, 73.91% of target configurations in S_9 are unreachable — a quantitative version of Bateson's clinical observation that most problems cannot be solved at the level they appear. Escape requires promotion to a higher operator class, with the required tier determined by the invariant separating source from target.

witnesses.note: Canonical dynamic T. (1,1) -> (1,2) stays in cosmos orbit 0. Orbit-preserving = Level I.
Scalar multiplication by 2 is an element of (Z/9Z)*, order 6. Sends cosmos orbit 0 to cosmos orbit 1 (rep (1,3) contains (2,2)). Orbit-changing, family-preserving = Level II-a.
Swap (b,e)->(e,b). (1,2) in orbit 0, (2,1) in orbit 1. Orbit-changing, family-preserving = Level II-a. Second II-a witness to confirm the class is non-degenerate.
Scalar multiplication by 3. 3 is NOT a unit mod 9 (gcd(3,9)=3). (1,1) cosmos -> (3,3) satellite. Family-changing = Level II-b.
Constant map to (9,9). Collapses every point (except the singularity itself) to the fixed point. Cosmos -> singularity is family-changing = Level II-b.

Reduction S_9 -> S_3. Codomain is a different state space entirely — cardinality 9 vs 81. No L_1 or L_2 operator can produce this (they are all self-maps of S_9). This is the canonical witness for L_2 ⊊ L_3.

strictness_verification.L_1_strict_in_L_2a: scalar_mult k=2 has 72/81 orbit crossings but 0 family crossings -> L_2a but not L_1. Witnessed.
strictness_verification.L_2a_strict_in_L_2b: scalar_mult k=3 has family crossings -> L_2b but not L_2a (L_2a requires family preservation). Witnessed.
strictness_verification.L_2b_strict_in_L_3: modulus_reduction with m_target=3 has codomain S_3, which is a different set from S_9. No self-map of S_9 (L_1 or L_2) can have codomain S_3. Witnessed by cardinality argument.
strictness_verification.chain: L_0 ⊊ L_1 ⊊ L_2a ⊊ L_2b ⊊ L_3, each inclusion strict by explicit witness.

convergence_map.source: Ashby ultrastability (Level 1 feedback / Level 2 parameter change)

cross_references.name: QA Origin of 24
cross_references.relation: Orbit classification via v_3(f) underpins the cosmos/satellite/singularity family distinction used here

cross_references.name: QA Eisenstein Norm
cross_references.relation: Norm f(b,e) = b²+be-e² determines orbit family

cross_references.name: QA Conic Discriminant
cross_references.relation: I>0/I=0/I<0 stratification is another invariant filtration on S_m

cross_references.name: QA Septenary Unit Group
cross_references.relation: (Z/9Z)* = 6-element group whose action on S_9 provides concrete L_2a operators

cross_references.name: QA Keely Triune
cross_references.relation: Triune (cosmos/satellite/singularity) = the family invariant preserved by L_2a, broken by L_2b
